title: Living in Boulder vlog 4 - 1 - 2017: 2,500 kilometer diameter backgrounds

enrichment.summary: Kyle records a late-night vlog entry for April 1, 2017, noting he woke up at 11am and stayed up until 2:30am. He describes working on a 'race distance grid' or 'circle grid' project involving geographic distances, which he expects to finish the next day, and briefly mentions starting a Google Analytics tutorial.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Very excited about it; sees it as a tool for understanding size, scale, and how political/cultural groupings form
Aware he is on a consistent late-night schedule but not concerned about it

transcript.text: Hello. Living in Boulder Vlog for April 1st, 2017. Right now it's April 2nd at 2.31 a.m.
Okay, so today I think I got up at 11, which was the same as all of the other days.
And I think I was up until like this time yesterday. So I'm on the same path. Same schedule.
I was working on my Google Earth thing, the race distance grid. And I was building more distances. It's a lot more intricate than I originally planned. I'm designing it as I'm going along.
And I'm deciding to, yeah, make it complete. I'm really excited to see. I think I'll be done with it tomorrow.
I'll be done with a grid tomorrow. And it'll be, you know, ready for checking it out on the cell phone.
I'm super excited about that. I think I want to include, though, I wouldn't call it the distance grid, though.
I'd call it the circle grid, because then I could put all the circles on the left. I was going to try that to see how it fit.
I'm curious to see, like, who likes it more, the stoners or the sobers? Because it seems to me super astounding.
Like, like, what it is, it's just understanding size for, so you can then plug it in to understanding political groups, groupings of people,
And exactly how it became that they were organized, how they are, you know, and they are, how they're, like, different languages and tribes of that size.
But anyway, I'll talk about that later. Yeah, it's super, super exciting. Oh, tonight I decided to start listening to Google Analytics tutorial.
That's interesting. Wasn't I listening to something else, though?
I remember, it must have all been that. I'm really hot. Yeah, but tomorrow I'll be done.
It may be even done with the page. What day is it? Sunday? Okay, so it's the weekend. Wow, it was already Saturday.
Okay, so yeah, I'll just treat it like a weekend thing. So I'll just try to finish tomorrow, and then I'll move on.
I'll back up and go back to the surface level. But tomorrow, yeah, I can finish the whole.
Page, because that was pretty much doing the Google Earth stuff was pretty much the whole thing, or all I need to do.
So I guess that's all I have to say.
Today was kind of a show on backgrounds.
Our, um,
Uh, the, let's see, we did 12s,
Are going to be the 24 to 50s.
And sorry if it's making people dizzy,
But we're almost through.
Okay, peace out.
